commands.review_summary:
Adds a shared Android notification app picker helper, wires an App Filter panel into the current notification settings screen, removes the duplicated helper from the legacy settings sheet, and adds picker filtering tests.

PR surface: Other +230. Total +230 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: open the current Android Settings > Notifications route on main and the screen has forwarding mode plus selected-app count but no picker controls. I did not run an emulator in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor-supplied inspectable real behavior proof plus normal maintainer/CI review; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(android): add notification app picker
target.body:
## Summary

- Restores a visible notification app picker in the current Android notification settings screen.
- Lets users search installed apps, include system packages when needed, and explicitly toggle selected packages for Blocklist or Allowlist mode.
- Moves the shared notification package query helpers out of the legacy settings sheet so both the current screen and tests use the same picker model.
- Keeps notification forwarding semantics unchanged: selection only updates `notifications.forwarding.packages`; it does not auto-enable forwarding or change listener/permission flow.
- AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the code path, kept the change scoped, and ran the validation below.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes none.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related none found. I checked the open Android-labeled PRs/issues before opening this PR and did not find a direct overlap for the notification app picker.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request. This is a narrow UI regression fix: the notification settings exposed Blocklist/Allowlist mode and a selected-app count, but the current settings screen did not expose a way to choose apps.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Android `Settings > Notifications` now exposes an `App Filter` panel with `Open App Picker`, app search, `Show System Apps`, app rows, and switches that persist selected package names.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw Android third-party debug build on a local API 35 emulator.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Built and installed the third-party debug APK.
  - Opened `Settings > Notifications` in the app.
  - Expanded `App Filter` with `Open App Picker`.
  - Searched apps, toggled `Show System Apps`, selected Calendar, verified the selected-app count and stored package value, then cleared the test selection.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - UI smoke showed these controls in the notification screen: `App Filter`, `Open App Picker`, `Search apps`, `Show System Apps`, app rows, and package switches.
  - Storage check after selecting Calendar showed `notifications.forwarding.packages` persisted the selected package; the test selection was cleared afterward.
  - Focused regression coverage was added for keeping selected system apps visible and matching by package-name search.
- Observed result after fix: users can now explicitly add or remove apps for the notification Blocklist/Allowlist from the current settings screen.
- What was not tested: a physical Android phone, Play flavor build, and background notification delivery from a real third-party app after changing the filter.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: screenshots were captured locally during the emulator smoke; no public artifact is attached to this PR.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this change, the current notification settings screen only showed forwarding mode plus selected-app count and did not expose an app picker.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `cd apps/android && ./gradlew :app:testThirdPartyDebugUnitTest :app:assembleThirdPartyDebug` failed first because `ANDROID_HOME` / `ANDROID_SDK_ROOT` were not set in the shell environment.
- `cd apps/android && ANDROID_HOME=<local Android SDK> ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=<local Android SDK> ./gradlew :app:testThirdPartyDebugUnitTest :app:assembleThirdPartyDebug` passed after rebasing onto current `upstream/main`.
- Local emulator smoke passed before the final rebase: install third-party debug build, open notification settings, expand the app picker, search/toggle system apps, select Calendar, verify persisted package selection, then clear it.
- `codex review --base upstream/main` was attempted, but local Codex auth returned `401 Unauthorized` before a review could complete.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added tests for `filterNotificationAppsForPicker` so selected system packages stay visible when system apps are hidden.
- Added tests for search matching against both app labels and package names.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The current notification settings screen had no user-facing app picker even though notification package filtering mode and selected package count were present.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A. Focused unit coverage was added for the picker filtering behavior.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The notification settings screen now shows an app filter panel and picker controls.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. It writes the same notification forwarding package CSV through the existing view model API.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. Notification listener access, Android notification permission, gateway connection, auth, secrets, network, and tool execution behavior are unchanged.

What is the highest-risk area?

Notification filtering UX: avoiding hidden selected packages and avoiding accidental auto-selection.

How is that risk mitigated?

Selected packages stay visible even when system apps are hidden, search covers labels and package names, and the picker only changes packages when the user toggles an explicit app switch.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review. A physical-phone notification-delivery smoke would be useful if maintainers want device-level proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet.
